definition: Every candle IS a micro dealing range with its own BSL (high), SSL (low), and equilibrium (50%).
depends_on: dealing_range (CRT applies dealing_range theory to individual candles)
liquidity (candle high/low are micro liquidity levels)
feeds_into: intraday_profile (previous candle's range defines next candle's liquidity targets
related_to: power_of_three_and_AMD (daily candle PO3: open=accumulation, wick=manipulation, close=distribution)
dealing_range (CRT IS dealing_range at candle level)
bridge_integration: Multi-TF CRT integrated across W1, D1, H4, M15, Wed-PO3, and SessionCRT (commits 82f8f51, 9c1be34, b32476a, 504d8a9, 057694c, ab4453d, plus the SessionCRT commit on 2026-04-26 — Followup B). detect_crt (Desktop/trading-ai-v2/analysis/ict/advanced.py:232) accepts a tf_label parameter (default 'M15') tagged onto each returned CRTSetup. detect_session_crt (same file, ~line 308) is a separate engine — session candles aren't time-equispaced bars, so it slices Asian (20:00–24:00 NY prev day) and London (02:00–10:00 NY today) by hour math directly and emits 0 or 1 SessionCRTSetup per call when London swept exactly one Asian extreme AND closed back inside the Asian range. The bridge calls detect_crt up to FIVE times per cycle in ict_pipeline.py step 8f (~line 893): W1 on df_w1[:-1], D1 on df_d1[:-1], H4 on df_htf_closed, plus the existing M15 call inside run_advanced_analysis, plus a conditional Wed-PO3 call when df_d1.index[-1].weekday()==2 (a 3-bar [Mon, Tue, Wed_live] window with lookback=1, tf_label='WedPO3'). detect_session_crt is called only when session_info.session is in (NY_OPEN, NY_AM, NY_PM, OVERLAP) — the distribution-leg window — over the trailing 96 M15 bars (~24h). Per-TF setups aggregate into adv.crt_setups; factors emit as CRT_W1(N), CRT_D1(N), CRT_H4(N), CRT_M15(N), CRT_WedPO3(N), CRT_SessionCRT(N). Score formula (ict_pipeline.py ~line 1230) uses per-TF weighting: CRT_W1=+5 (institutional swing reversal at PWH/PWL), CRT_D1=+4 (major reversal), CRT_WedPO3=+3 (Wednesday manipulation), CRT_H4=+3 (swing-tradable), CRT_SessionCRT=+3 (session-bounded; London swept Asian + close back inside; NY draws toward opposite Asian extreme), CRT_M15=+2 (intrabar); other advanced_factors stay at +2.5; total still capped at +10. Backtest gates: scripts/bench_multi_tf_crt.py confirms W1/D1/H4/M15 weights stay under the +/-2.0 ship gate across 5 symbols / 1260 cycles. scripts/bench_session_crt.py confirms SessionCRT fires in 22.0% of NY-window cycles (642/2912 across XAUUSD/EURUSD/GBPUSD/BTCUSD/ETHUSD), with a balanced bullish/bearish split (XAUUSD 58/68, EURUSD 57/70, GBPUSD 64/52, BTCUSD 67/80, ETHUSD 64/62) — no systemic directional bias. Wed-PO3 fires only ~14% of trading days; no dedicated bench needed. Concept injector (concept_injector.py:259-298) emits TF-differentiated hints with priority W1 > D1 > WedPO3 > SessionCRT > H4 > M15 — 'Weekly CRT — institutional swing-trade reversal at PWH/PWL', 'Daily CRT — major reversal', 'Wednesday-PO3 — institutional manipulation day; Thursday distribution likely opposite direction', 'Session-CRT — London swept one side of Asian range and closed back inside; NY targets opposite Asian extreme', 'H4 CRT — swing-tradable', 'M15 CRT — intrabar confluence'. Existing synergies: MultiTF_CRT (+5) fires when CRT_D1 and CRT_H4 both present (synergy_scorer.py:_multi_tf_crt); SessionCRT+KillZone (+4) fires when CRT_SessionCRT factor present AND we're inside an NY kill zone (NY_OPEN/NY_AM/NY_PM/OVERLAP per _in_ny_kill_zone helper). NOT yet enforced as a hard gate (Grade A/B can still clear without CRT). Still NOT integrated: MultiTF_CRT_W1_D1 super-synergy (very rare, deferred), counter-D1-CRT trade gate (defer to ≥30 counter-CRT trades in journal). Claude's prompt (claude_decision.py:884-886) renders all CRT_* factors on the Advanced ICT line plus the injected card+hint text.
common_mistakes: Expecting BOTH extremes to be swept in one session. The algorithm typically sweeps only ONE side per candle cycle. If the high is taken first, expect the low to be the target — do not wait for both sweeps before entering.

fractal_application.definition: Every timeframe's candle is a complete dealing range. CRT applies identically across all timeframes — the rules do not change, only the scale.
fractal_application.daily_candle: PDH = BSL target. PDL = SSL target. 50% = equilibrium for current day. Once one extreme is swept, the opposite extreme becomes the draw.
fractal_application.weekly_candle: PWH = weekly BSL. PWL = weekly SSL. Once one weekly extreme is swept, opposite weekly extreme is the target for the remaining sessions.
fractal_application.4h_candle: Previous 4H high/low define the next 4H session's liquidity targets. Treat each 4H candle as a mini dealing range.
fractal_application.target_selection_rule: Once a candle's HIGH is swept (BSL taken), the LOW becomes the next target (SSL). Once the LOW is swept, the HIGH becomes the target. This alternation is the fractal engine.

weekly_PO3.definition: The weekly candle itself follows Power of Three — the 5-session week has its own accumulation, manipulation, and distribution phases.
weekly_PO3.monday: Accumulation. Monday typically establishes the opening range. Low conviction directional movement. Range building.
weekly_PO3.tuesday: Continuation of accumulation or first reversal signal. Often Monday's range is tested. Sets up Wednesday.
weekly_PO3.wednesday: MANIPULATION. Highest probability day for a weekly extreme sweep. Wednesday most commonly sweeps Monday's high OR Monday's low, triggering the weekly manipulation phase.
weekly_PO3.thursday: Distribution begins. After Wednesday's manipulation (sweep), Thursday delivers the REAL move in the opposite direction. Highest conviction directional day.
weekly_PO3.friday: Reduced volume. Partial consolidation of Thursday's distribution. Often prices close near the opposite weekly extreme from Wednesday's sweep. Avoid new entries.

candle_anatomy_as_PO3.open: The candle open IS the accumulation price. The opening price is where institutional algorithms begin building position.
candle_anatomy_as_PO3.first_wick: The initial wick (against the eventual close direction) IS the manipulation phase. Price briefly moves wrong to sweep stops.
candle_anatomy_as_PO3.body: The candle body IS the distribution phase. The body from open to close represents the real institutional delivery.
candle_anatomy_as_PO3.closing_wick: The wick at the close end IS the preview for the NEXT candle's manipulation target. If price closes bullish with a long upper wick, the next candle will likely sweep that wick high.

session_candle_theory.definition: Each trading session's candle (Asian, London, NY) is a CRT fractal at the session level. Implemented in detect_session_crt (Desktop/trading-ai-v2/analysis/ict/advanced.py ~line 308) — the bridge calls it from ict_pipeline.py step 8f only during NY-window cycles (NY_OPEN/NY_AM/NY_PM/OVERLAP) and emits CRT_SessionCRT(N) when London swept exactly one Asian extreme AND closed back inside the Asian range.
session_candle_theory.asian_candle: Asian session candle = accumulation / reference range (20:00–24:00 NY prev day). asian_high = BSL target, asian_low = SSL target. Bars carrying the Asian aggregate are paired to the *next* NY trading date so they line up with that date's London and NY sessions.
session_candle_theory.london_candle: London session candle = manipulation (02:00–10:00 NY today). London almost always sweeps one Asian extreme. The detector fires only when London swept exactly ONE side AND london_close lands back inside the Asian range. Both-side sweeps emit nothing (ambiguous); a London close OUTSIDE the Asian range emits nothing (sweep didn't reverse).
session_candle_theory.ny_candle: NY session candle = distribution (07:00–17:00 NY today). The SessionCRTSetup direction is OPPOSITE the London swept side: London-swept-low → bullish NY draw toward asian_high; London-swept-high → bearish NY draw toward asian_low. SessionCRT+KillZone synergy (+4 in synergy_scorer.py) fires when this lands inside an NY kill zone, the entry-window confluence.

application.daily_candle: PDH = BSL target, PDL = SSL target, 50% = equilibrium for today's activity.
application.weekly_candle: Mon-Tue establish range. Wed typically sweeps one extreme (manipulation).

how_to_trade.step_1: Identify the prior candle's high (BSL) and low (SSL) on your operating timeframe.
how_to_trade.step_2: Determine which side is the current draw based on HTF bias.
how_to_trade.step_3: Wait for price to sweep the manipulation side first (often opposite of the real move).
how_to_trade.step_4: After the sweep, enter in the direction of the draw. SL beyond the swept extreme.
how_to_trade.example_bullish: After PDH is swept (BSL taken with a wick above), price reverses. PDL becomes the SSL target (draw). Enter long on displacement after the PDH sweep.
how_to_trade.example_bearish: After PDL is swept (SSL taken), PDH becomes the BSL target. Enter short on displacement after the PDL sweep.

connection: CRT_candle_range_theory is dealing_range + liquidity + PO3 applied fractally to every single candle. It unifies these three concepts at the smallest scale.
